wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# PromptFoo: Comprehensive LLM Evaluation Toolkit

## Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
PromptFoo is a comprehensive **Large Language Model (LLM) evaluation and testing toolkit** designed to help developers, researchers, and organizations validate, benchmark, and improve their AI applications. The project provides a unified framework for evaluating LLM outputs across multiple providers, assertions, and evaluation criteria.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Universal LLM Evaluation**: Support for multiple AI providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, AWS Bedrock, and others
- **Flexible Assertion System**: Comprehensive built-in assertions for quality, safety, and performance evaluation
- **Multi-format Support**: YAML/JSON configuration, CSV datasets, and programmatic APIs
- **Extensible Architecture**: Custom provider integration and assertion development capabilities
- **Production Ready**: Robust caching, rate limiting, and distributed evaluation support

### Target Audience
- AI/ML engineers building LLM applications
- DevOps teams implementing CI/CD for AI systems
- Researchers validating model performance
- Organizations requiring compliance and safety testing

## Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[CLI Interface] --> B[Evaluation Engine]
    B --> C[Provider Registry]
    B --> D[Assertion System]
    B --> E[Configuration Parser]
    
    C --> F[OpenAI Provider]
    C --> G[Anthropic Provider]
    C --> H[AWS Bedrock Provider]
    C --> I[Custom Providers]
    
    D --> J[Built-in Assertions]
    D --> K[Custom Assertions]
    D --> L[Model-graded Assertions]
    
    E --> M[YAML Config]
    E --> N[JSON Config]
    E --> O[Programmatic API]
    
    B --> P[Database Layer]
    B --> Q[Cache System]
    B --> R[Telemetry]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Factory Pattern**: Provider registration and instantiation
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable assertion systems
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven evaluation lifecycle
- **Command Pattern**: CLI command structure
- **Template Method**: Evaluation pipeline orchestration

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant CLI as CLI Interface
    participant EVAL as Evaluation Engine
    participant PROV as Provider Layer
    participant ASSERT as Assertion System
    participant DB as Database
    
    CLI->>+EVAL: promptfoo eval
    EVAL->>+PROV: Load Providers
    PROV-->>-EVAL: Provider Instances
    EVAL->>+ASSERT: Process Assertions
    ASSERT-->>-EVAL: Evaluation Results
    EVAL->>+DB: Store Results
    DB-->>-EVAL: Persist Confirmation
    EVAL-->>-CLI: Final Report
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **CLI Entrypoint**: `src/entrypoint.ts` - Node.js version validation and module loading
- **Application Entry**: `src/main.ts` - Command-line interface and main application logic
- **Library Export**: `src/index.ts` - Programmatic API for integration

### Core Modules
- **Evaluation Engine**: `src/evaluator.ts` - Central evaluation orchestrator with progress tracking
- **Provider System**: `src/providers/index.ts` - Dynamic provider loading and resolution
- **Configuration Types**: `src/types/index.ts` - Comprehensive type definitions and validation schemas
- **Database Models**: `src/models/eval.ts` - ORM models for evaluation results and metadata

### Configuration Approach
- **YAML/JSON Config**: `examples/getting-started/promptfooconfig.yaml` - Primary configuration format
- **Environment Variables**: Flexible environment-based configuration
- **Programmatic API**: Direct JavaScript/TypeScript integration

### External Dependencies
- **Provider SDKs**: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google AI, AWS SDKs
- **Database**: Better-SQLite3 for local storage, Drizzle ORM for schema management
- **CLI Framework**: Commander.js for command-line interface
- **Validation**: Zod for configuration schema validation

### Integration Points
- **Cloud Providers**: AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, Google AI Studio
- **Observability**: OpenTelemetry integration for distributed tracing
- **Storage**: Filesystem and cloud storage backends
- **CI/CD**: GitHub Actions integration and Docker containerization

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class Eval {
        +create()
        +run()
        +getResult()
    }
    
    class Provider {
        +callApi()
        +id()
        +label
    }
    
    class Assertion {
        +execute()
        +getDescription()
    }
    
    class TestCase {
        +vars
        +assert
        +metadata
    }
    
    Eval --> Provider : uses
    Eval --> Assertion : applies
    Eval --> TestCase : evaluates
    Provider --> Eval : responds to
    Assertion --> TestCase : validates
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

#### Multi-Provider Support
- **Universal Provider Interface**: Consistent API across different AI providers
- **Dynamic Loading**: Runtime provider discovery and instantiation
- **Configuration Flexibility**: Environment-based provider configuration

#### Comprehensive Assertion System
- **Built-in Assertions**: Contains, equals, similarity, moderation, and many more
- **Custom Assertions**: Extensible assertion framework for domain-specific validation
- **Model-Graded Evaluations**: LLM-as-a-judge capabilities for subjective assessments

#### Advanced Evaluation Capabilities
- **Red Team Testing**: Systematic adversarial testing framework
- **Multi-turn Conversations**: Complex dialogue evaluation support
- **Streaming Support**: Real-time evaluation of streaming responses

### Implementation Highlights

#### Evaluation Pipeline (`src/evaluator.ts`)
- Concurrent evaluation with configurable limits
- Progress tracking and reporting
- Caching mechanisms for performance optimization
- Distributed evaluation support

#### Provider Architecture (`src/providers/index.ts`)
- Factory-based provider creation
- Environment variable templating
- Cloud provider integration
- Custom provider development framework

#### Configuration Management (`src/types/index.ts`)
- Zod-based schema validation
- Type-safe configuration objects
- Extensible configuration system

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> LoadingConfig
    LoadingConfig --> LoadingProviders
    LoadingProviders --> ProcessingTests
    ProcessingTests --> RunningAssertions
    RunningAssertions --> GeneratingReports
    GeneratingReports --> [*]
    
    ProcessingTests --> CachingResults
    CachingResults --> RunningAssertions
    
    RunningAssertions --> ErrorHandling
    ErrorHandling --> RunningAssertions
    ErrorHandling --> GeneratingReports
```

The project demonstrates a mature, production-ready architecture for LLM evaluation with extensive provider support, flexible configuration, and robust evaluation capabilities. Its modular design enables easy extension and customization while maintaining high performance and reliability.

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [src/index.ts](src/index.ts)
- [src/entrypoint.ts](src/entrypoint.ts)
- [src/main.ts](src/main.ts)
- [src/evaluator.ts](src/evaluator.ts)
- [src/providers/index.ts](src/providers/index.ts)
- [src/types/index.ts](src/types/index.ts)
- [src/models/eval.ts](src/models/eval.ts)
- [examples/getting-started/promptfooconfig.yaml](examples/getting-started/promptfooconfig.yaml)

</blog>
